what_it_does: Stores YAML front-matter metadata for a channel-memory attachment that points to an image asset.
observations: The file contains only YAML front matter and no body text.
It declares an attachment of type 'images'.
The target is an image:// URI pointing to design-source/recent-pitches/p-09.
The id uses an 'att-' prefix with a hash suffix.
standouts: The target is a namespaced image URI (image://design-source/recent-pitches/p-09).
conventions: Uses YAML front matter with a fixed set of metadata fields (id, channel, attachment_type, target, added).
The target URI uses a channel-scoped image:// scheme.
concerns: No body or caption explains what the image contains.
The image URI cannot be verified from this file alone.
There is no recorded author or provenance for the attachment.
notes: A bare attachment record that links the design-source channel memory to a specific pitch image.
questions: What visual content does image://design-source/recent-pitches/p-09 contain?
Who added this attachment and why?
Is the target image still current or has it been superseded?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches: the file is meant to record an attachment reference, and it does exactly that.
intention_signals: To register an image from the recent-pitches collection as a retrievable attachment under the design-source channel.
contribution: Provides a typed, timestamped pointer from a channel memory entry to an image asset, supporting asset retrieval and association without embedding the asset itself.
summary_for_embedding: A YAML-only channel-memory attachment record of type images that references image://design-source/recent-pitches/p-09 and records an added timestamp.
intention_for_embedding: To associate a recent pitch image with the design-source channel memory for later lookup.
